Class GameViewRenderer

java.lang.Object
com.comp2042.view.GameViewRenderer
public class GameViewRenderer extends Object
Centralized rendering class for all game view rendering operations. Handles drawing of bricks, ghost pieces, board background, next bricks, and hold bricks.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    GameViewRenderer()
     

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    clearBrickPanels(MultiplayerScreen screen)
    Clears all brick panels for multiplayer (brick panels, ghost panels, hold panels, next panels).
    void
    clearDisplayMatrix(javafx.scene.shape.Rectangle[][] displayMatrix)
    Clears the display matrix by setting all cells to transparent.
    void
    clearNextBrickPanes(List<javafx.scene.layout.GridPane> nextBrickPanes, int brickSize)
    Clears and re-initializes next brick panes with transparent rectangles.
    void
    refreshBrick(ViewData brick, javafx.scene.layout.GridPane brickPanel, javafx.scene.layout.GridPane ghostPanel, InputEventListener eventListener, int scaledBrickSize)
    Renders the current brick and ghost piece on the game board.
    void
    refreshBrick(MultiplayerScreen screen, ViewData brick, int playerNumber)
    Refreshes the brick display for a multiplayer player.
    void
    refreshGameBackground(int[][] board, javafx.scene.shape.Rectangle[][] displayMatrix)
    Refreshes the game board background by updating the display matrix colors.
    void
    refreshGameBackground(MultiplayerScreen screen, int[][] board, int playerNumber)
    Refreshes the game background for a multiplayer player.
    void
    renderHoldBrick(Brick heldBrick, javafx.scene.shape.Rectangle[][] holdBrickRectangles)
    Renders the held brick in the hold panel.
    void

    Updates the hold brick display for a multiplayer player.
    void
    updateNextBricks(MultiplayerScreen screen, List<Brick> nextBricks, int playerNumber)
    Updates the next bricks display for a multiplayer player.
    void

    Updates the next bricks preview display.

  • Constructor Details

    • GameViewRenderer

      public GameViewRenderer()

  • Method Details

    • refreshBrick

      public void refreshBrick(ViewData brick, javafx.scene.layout.GridPane brickPanel, javafx.scene.layout.GridPane ghostPanel, InputEventListener eventListener, int scaledBrickSize)
      Renders the current brick and ghost piece on the game board.
      Parameters:
      brick - The brick data to render
      brickPanel - The GridPane to draw the brick on
      ghostPanel - The GridPane to draw the ghost piece on (can be null)
      eventListener - The event listener to get board state for ghost position
      scaledBrickSize - The scaled brick size (for multiplayer scaling)

    • refreshGameBackground

      public void refreshGameBackground(int[][] board, javafx.scene.shape.Rectangle[][] displayMatrix)
      Refreshes the game board background by updating the display matrix colors.
      Parameters:
      board - The board matrix to render
      displayMatrix - The Rectangle matrix representing the board cells

    • updateNextBricks

      public void updateNextBricks(List<Brick> nextBricks, List<javafx.scene.layout.GridPane> nextBrickPanes, int brickSize, int maxBricks)
      Updates the next bricks preview display.
      Parameters:
      nextBricks - The list of next bricks to display
      nextBrickPanes - The list of GridPanes to render bricks in
      brickSize - The size of each brick cell
      maxBricks - The maximum number of bricks to display

    • renderHoldBrick

      public void renderHoldBrick(Brick heldBrick, javafx.scene.shape.Rectangle[][] holdBrickRectangles)
      Renders the held brick in the hold panel.
      Parameters:
      heldBrick - The brick to render (can be null to clear)
      holdBrickRectangles - The Rectangle matrix for the hold panel

    • clearDisplayMatrix

      public void clearDisplayMatrix(javafx.scene.shape.Rectangle[][] displayMatrix)
      Clears the display matrix by setting all cells to transparent.
      Parameters:
      displayMatrix - The Rectangle matrix to clear

    • clearNextBrickPanes

      public void clearNextBrickPanes(List<javafx.scene.layout.GridPane> nextBrickPanes, int brickSize)
      Clears and re-initializes next brick panes with transparent rectangles.
      Parameters:
      nextBrickPanes - The list of GridPanes to clear and re-initialize
      brickSize - The size of each brick cell

    • refreshBrick

      public void refreshBrick(MultiplayerScreen screen, ViewData brick, int playerNumber)
      Refreshes the brick display for a multiplayer player.
      Parameters:
      screen - The MultiplayerScreen instance
      brick - The brick data to render
      playerNumber - The player number (1 or 2)

    • refreshGameBackground

      public void refreshGameBackground(MultiplayerScreen screen, int[][] board, int playerNumber)
      Refreshes the game background for a multiplayer player.
      Parameters:
      screen - The MultiplayerScreen instance
      board - The board matrix to render
      playerNumber - The player number (1 or 2)

    • updateNextBricks

      public void updateNextBricks(MultiplayerScreen screen, List<Brick> nextBricks, int playerNumber)
      Updates the next bricks display for a multiplayer player.
      Parameters:
      screen - The MultiplayerScreen instance
      nextBricks - The list of next bricks to display
      playerNumber - The player number (1 or 2)

    • updateHoldBrick

      public void updateHoldBrick(MultiplayerScreen screen, Brick heldBrick, int playerNumber)
      Updates the hold brick display for a multiplayer player.
      Parameters:
      screen - The MultiplayerScreen instance
      heldBrick - The brick to render (can be null to clear)
      playerNumber - The player number (1 or 2)

    • clearBrickPanels

      public void clearBrickPanels(MultiplayerScreen screen)
      Clears all brick panels for multiplayer (brick panels, ghost panels, hold panels, next panels).
      Parameters:
      screen - The MultiplayerScreen instance
